limitation_note: Ideal spaced subset was epochs 21/30/40/50 (K=4); only epoch 21 and 50 survived Kaggle-to-Vast.ai migration. K=2 used instead.
benchmark_comparison: In-domain r=0.43 vs >90% (conformal QR, arXiv:2601.13236) and R=0.94 (NPB-REC, Kustner et al.) - honest cost-quality tradeoff for a zero-training-cost signal.
headline_finding: Checkpoint-ensemble uncertainty is a moderate in-domain error predictor but a strong, statistically robust OOD/distribution-shift detector (1.55x shift, p<1e-41), making it directly usable as a drift-monitor validation signal even though its per-slice error correlation breaks down under domain shift.
